column1: # Aozora gaiji composition-description census (P39-5) — the HELD input for the
# aozora IDS lane builder (Nabu::Ops::AozoraIdsBuilder -> config/gaiji/aozora-ids.tsv).
# Each row is a DISTINCT component-composition description that the aozora-ruby
# parser (P38-3) left as a loud in-text sentinel ※［＃…］ + a class=="unresolved"
# gaiji annotation, because it carries neither a JIS kuten nor a U+ codepoint
# (survey §4c class-(c)). The "desc" is exactly the parser's stored gaiji "desc"
# field: the leading 「…」 component-formula (page/line locators and reading
# notes stripped), so it is the STABLE per-glyph key regardless of which book or
# page an occurrence came from — the same key the display render path derives
# from the in-text notation (Nabu::Display::GAIJI_SENTINELS["aozora"]).
# Rows are the composition subset ONLY (descs containing ＋ or ／): the input
# the IDS grammar can act on. The builder derives an IDS for the mechanical cases
# and REFUSES the rest (kana radical-names, subtraction −, 代えて replacements,
# parenthesised/nested or multi-operator formulae). count<TAB>desc; count = live
# occurrences at the census date.
# GENERATED (read-only) from db/catalog.sqlite3 — the parse-time gaiji_unresolved
# annotations of the live aozora corpus. A snapshot, regenerated on the owner's
# schedule as the corpus grows (mirrors the fold-table caveat).
# census: 582, 2026-07-22, aozora composition descriptions — 16004 documents,
#   1861 distinct unresolved-gaiji descriptions (19094 occurrences) total;
#   the composition subset below is 582 distinct (1129 occurrences).
# count
